 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.30.1","title":"Crash-safe lexical publish backup+recovery swap","description":"Slice ibuuh.30: replace the current remove_dir_all(index)+rename(staged,index) publish with a backup/swap flow that records enough recovery state to restore old-good on restart if a crash lands between rename steps. Wire startup/index open paths to recover the prior published index when the live path is missing but the staged-publish backup is present. Add regression coverage around publish recovery semantics.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-23T15:53:42.372059839Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:04:46.130019452Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:04:46.129628670Z","close_reason":"already landed in main as 109560e5 before this slice could ship; no-op duplicate child","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.30.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.30","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T15:53:42.372059839Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.31","title":"Define a versioned ConversationPacket contract so refresh normalizes once and feeds many sinks","description":"BACKGROUND:
The refresh pipeline repeatedly re-normalizes the same conversation for different sinks: canonical persistence, lexical indexing, analytics derivation, and semantic preparation. That duplicates CPU, allocations, and maintenance burden.

GOAL:
Define a versioned ConversationPacket contract that represents the canonical normalized unit of work for refresh and rebuild pipelines.

SCOPE:
- Define the packet schema and invariants for normalized content, provenance, timestamps, stable hashes, and sink-specific projections.
- Support packet construction from both raw connector scan output and canonical SQLite replay.
- Separate canonical payload from derived or cacheable fields so the packet stays memory-conscious and versionable.

DONE WHEN:
Cass has a clear normalize-once contract that later beads can adopt instead of re-deriving the same work in parallel codepaths.","acceptance_criteria":"ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- A documented, versioned ConversationPacket contract exists with invariants for normalized content, provenance, timestamps, stable hashes, and sink projections.
- Builders exist for both raw connector scan output and canonical SQLite replay, and fixture tests prove they produce equivalent packet semantics for the same logical conversation.
- The packet design stays memory-conscious by separating canonical payload from derived or cacheable fields, with logs or diagnostics that make packet version mismatches explicit.","notes":"LOCAL VALIDATION POLICY:
- This bead must land with bead-local proof, not only final-epic proof.
- Required by default: targeted unit tests for new invariants, integration tests for the affected runtime path, and at least one CLI or robot E2E scenario when the behavior is user-visible, multi-step, or crash-sensitive.
- Preserve rich structured timestamped logs, strategy or fallback reasons, and artifact snapshots relevant to the bead: manifests, benchmark tables, digests, checkpoints, crash-resume traces, controller decisions, or cache evidence.
- Prefer extending the shared harness from coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.15 and the crash or load harness from coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.17 instead of inventing ad hoc test entrypoints.
- Heavy cargo-driven builds, tests, benches, or profiling for this track must use rch.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:10.269604514Z","created_by":"RedCat","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:06:27.775990602Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T20:06:27.775503340Z","close_reason":"Defined the versioned ConversationPacket contract with raw and canonical builders, semantic equivalence tests, sink projections, and version diagnostics.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","architecture","indexing","semantic","storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.31","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:10.269604514Z","created_by":"RedCat","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.31","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:14.257351669Z","created_by":"RedCat","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":513,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.31","author":"RedCat","text":"DESIGN / ACCEPTANCE / NOTES:
- The packet must be rich enough for DB, lexical, analytics, and semantic sinks, but not so bloated that it becomes its own memory problem.
- Stable content hashes and version markers are part of the contract so memoization and equivalence work become straightforward later.
- This bead is about the contract and builder invariants first; full pipeline migration is intentionally deferred.
- Acceptance requires a documented, versioned packet contract that can be built from both raw scan output and canonical replay.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:10Z"},{"id":576,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.31","author":"ubuntu","text":"APRIL 2026 MANY-CORE INDEXING ADDENDUM

The existing ConversationPacket direction is correct, but the many-core indexing effort needs a stricter hot-path contract than the broader original bead implied. Fingerprinting, lexical slicing, provenance normalization, planner budgeting, and queue control all need one memory-conscious work unit that is explicit about identity, stable hashes, slices, and byte budgets.

This refinement is captured in child bead coding_agent_session_search-9fs8i, which defines the lightweight packet or projection that producer, worker, planner, and sink stages will share.

Intent: future pipeline work should not re-litigate field shape or drag sink-specific payload bloat through the hot path.","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:11:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.32","title":"Migrate refresh and rebuild flows onto ConversationPacket-driven dataflow","description":"BACKGROUND:
A packet contract only matters if the live refresh path actually uses it. To get the architectural payoff, the refresh and rebuild paths must stop normalizing independently for each sink and instead consume packet projections from one shared source of truth.

GOAL:
Migrate refresh and rebuild flows onto ConversationPacket-driven dataflow.

SCOPE:
- Make scan producers and canonical replay produce packets instead of sink-specific structs.
- Adapt canonical persistence, lexical builders, analytics derivation, and semantic preparation to consume packet projections.
- Remove duplicated transformation logic only after equivalence is proven.
- Preserve watch-mode and targeted incremental behavior while converging on the packet path.

DONE WHEN:
The main refresh path is packet-driven end-to-end and no longer re-normalizes the same conversation independently for each sink.","acceptance_criteria":"ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- The primary refresh path constructs packets once and feeds canonical persistence, lexical indexing, analytics, and semantic preparation from shared packet projections instead of repeated normalization passes.
- Migration happens incrementally with equivalence gates, explicit observability showing which paths are packet-driven versus legacy, and a temporary shadow or compare mode plus explicit kill-switch or demotion path so divergence can be diagnosed without trapping users on a broken path.
- Full refresh, watch-mode, targeted incremental updates, repair flows, and restart recovery remain correct after the migration, with unit, integration, and robot or E2E coverage for mixed old or new-path scenarios, preserved packet-versus-legacy diff artifacts, and at least one representative corpus run proving rollback to the legacy-safe path remains available until equivalence is established.","notes":"LOCAL VALIDATION POLICY:
- This bead must land with bead-local proof, not only final-epic proof.
- Required by default: targeted unit tests for new invariants, integration tests for the affected runtime path, and at least one CLI or robot E2E scenario when the behavior is user-visible, multi-step, or crash-sensitive.
- Preserve rich structured timestamped logs, strategy or fallback reasons, and artifact snapshots relevant to the bead: manifests, benchmark tables, digests, checkpoints, crash-resume traces, controller decisions, or cache evidence.
- Prefer extending the shared harness from coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.15 and the crash or load harness from coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.17 instead of inventing ad hoc test entrypoints.
- Heavy cargo-driven builds, tests, benches, or profiling for this track must use rch.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:10.672646617Z","created_by":"RedCat","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:36:56.548059753Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","indexing","lexical","semantic","storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.32","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:10.672646617Z","created_by":"RedCat","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.32","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:14.640786738Z","created_by":"RedCat","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.32","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.31","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:14.449179014Z","created_by":"RedCat","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":514,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.32","author":"RedCat","text":"DESIGN / ACCEPTANCE / NOTES:
- Migrate incrementally with equivalence gates; do not switch every sink at once without proof.
- Prefer shared projection helpers over sink-specific extraction code.
- Keep packet production and consumption observable so later agents can tell which paths still bypass the shared contract.
- Acceptance requires the primary refresh path to construct packets once and feed multiple sinks from those packet projections.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:10Z"},{"id":577,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.32","author":"ubuntu","text":"APRIL 2026 MANY-CORE INDEXING ADDENDUM

The new architecture cannot stop at full rebuild. Watch mode, import, salvage, and incremental repair paths must converge on the same packet-driven pipeline or cass will simply re-accumulate bespoke loops, serial hot spots, and correctness drift in different entrypoints.

This parent now has two explicit refinements:
- coding_agent_session_search-72sq9 converts full rebuild into a bounded producer-worker-consumer pipeline with observable backpressure and monotone checkpoints.
- coding_agent_session_search-tin8o migrates watch, import, salvage, and other meaningful entrypoints onto that same pipeline using shadow-equivalence gates before legacy loops are retired.

Intent: one shared streaming dataflow should become the authoritative indexing architecture across rebuild and incremental flows.","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:11:20Z"}]}
+{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.32.1","title":"[ibuuh.32] migrate analytics rebuild/repair onto ConversationPacket analytics projection","description":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.32 remains open because the semantic and lexical refresh paths are packet-driven, but the packet contract is not yet the authoritative source for analytics derivation. ConversationPacketSinkProjections.analytics exists in src/model/conversation_packet.rs, but production analytics rebuild/repair flows do not consume it yet.

Work:
- route analytics rebuild/repair entrypoints through the ConversationPacket analytics projection instead of re-deriving per-conversation counts independently
- add equivalence coverage proving packet analytics matches current canonical behavior on representative conversations
- keep a diagnosable fallback/demotion path until equivalence is established

Done when:
- the primary refresh path uses the packet contract for analytics as well as lexical and semantic sinks
- the remaining ibuuh.32 acceptance gap is closed with targeted tests and rollout-visible observability","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:39:48.815177429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:40:03.246638898Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","indexing","packet"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.32.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.32","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:39:48.815177429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.33","title":"Implement a deterministic parallel lexical segment-farm rebuild with safe fallback and atomic generation publish","description":"BACKGROUND:
Even a highly optimized serial lexical rebuild has a ceiling because one thread ultimately replays the corpus into the lexical writer. Once serial hot-path waste is removed and generation semantics exist, the next step toward world-class rebuild is to build lexical segments in parallel and publish one validated generation.

GOAL:
Implement a deterministic parallel lexical segment-farm rebuild with safe fallback and atomic generation publish.

SCOPE:
- Define deterministic shard planning using conversation, message, and byte budgets.
- Build lexical shard outputs in parallel with bounded worker concurrency.
- Validate shard outputs, assemble them into one publishable generation, and promote that generation atomically.
- Provide conservative fallback to the improved serial path when capability gaps or resource limits make parallel rebuild unsafe.
- Extend frankensearch upstream if the required segment import or assembly primitives are missing.

DONE WHEN:
Cass can rebuild lexical state through a validated parallel segment-farm path, publish one generation atomically, and fall back safely when the environment cannot support the fast path.","acceptance_criteria":"ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Deterministic shard planning exists with explicit budgets for conversations, messages, bytes, and worker concurrency, and the same input corpus produces the same shard plan.
- Parallel shard outputs are validated and assembled into exactly one publishable generation; partial success never becomes published success, and safe fallback to the improved serial path is explicit and well logged.
- Benchmark and crash-resume evidence show materially improved large-corpus rebuild performance over the improved serial path without sacrificing determinism, publish safety, or diagnosability.","notes":"LOCAL VALIDATION POLICY:
- This bead must land with bead-local proof, not only final-epic proof.
- Required by default: targeted unit tests for new invariants, integration tests for the affected runtime path, and at least one CLI or robot E2E scenario when the behavior is user-visible, multi-step, or crash-sensitive.
- Preserve rich structured timestamped logs, strategy or fallback reasons, and artifact snapshots relevant to the bead: manifests, benchmark tables, digests, checkpoints, crash-resume traces, controller decisions, or cache evidence.
- Prefer extending the shared harness from coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.15 and the crash or load harness from coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.17 instead of inventing ad hoc test entrypoints.
- Heavy cargo-driven builds, tests, benches, or profiling for this track must use rch.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:11.086423028Z","created_by":"RedCat","updated_at":"2026-04-23T01:37:57.873214676Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T01:37:57.872900629Z","close_reason":"Added simulation evidence for deterministic segment-farm shard planning, validation, atomic publish crash fallback, and retry publish.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["indexing","lexical","parallelism","performance","tantivy"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.33","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:11.086423028Z","created_by":"RedCat","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.33","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:18.126234709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.33","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.30","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:14.836764450Z","created_by":"RedCat","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.33","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.31","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:15.034083288Z","created_by":"RedCat","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.33","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.32","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:15.229856627Z","created_by":"RedCat","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":515,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.33","author":"RedCat","text":"DESIGN / ACCEPTANCE / NOTES:
- Determinism matters. Parallelism should improve throughput without making outputs or bugs impossible to reason about.
- Published generations should be assembled only from validated shard artifacts; partial success is not publishable success.
- Keep rollback simple: if validation, assembly, or publish fails, preserve the previously published generation and surface the failure clearly.
- Acceptance requires deterministic shard planning, bounded worker concurrency, atomic publish, safe fallback, and measurable large-corpus improvement over the improved serial path.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:11Z"},{"id":545,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.33","author":"ubuntu","text":"POLISH ROUND 8:
- Added a direct dependency on coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.29 because the parallel segment-farm path is only credible if it has a verified improved serial fallback beneath it.
- This keeps the architecture legible: parallel rebuild is an upgrade over the serial path, not a separate universe with a different safety story.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:56:03Z"},{"id":579,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.33","author":"ubuntu","text":"APRIL 2026 MANY-CORE INDEXING ADDENDUM

The remaining lexical bottleneck is architectural: one writer or merge path cannot satisfy the user requirement of many-core end-to-end indexing on modern machines. This epic is therefore decomposed into an explicit shard-farm plan:
- coding_agent_session_search-a9698: deterministic shard planning and work budgeting
- coding_agent_session_search-zbu32: parallel shard builders plus independent shard validation
- coding_agent_session_search-2uotv: query fan-out and deterministic merge across shard generations
- coding_agent_session_search-vamq7: deferred background compaction and explicit merge-debt accounting

Intent: use many cores safely during lexical rebuild, preserve deterministic search semantics, and stop large merge debt from creeping back onto the critical path.","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:11:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34","title":"Add content-addressed memoization for expensive refresh derivations keyed by stable packet hashes","description":"BACKGROUND:
Once the pipeline is packet-driven, cass can stop recomputing identical derived work for repeated content. Historical salvage, replayed sessions, repeated assistant boilerplate, and semantic rebuilds all create opportunities for content-addressed reuse.

GOAL:
Add content-addressed memoization for expensive refresh derivations keyed by stable packet hashes.

SCOPE:
- Key memoization by stable content hash plus algorithm or version fingerprint.
- Reuse cached results for lexical normalization artifacts, token extraction or model normalization, semantic embeddings, and other demonstrably expensive pure derivations.
- Keep cache storage, invalidation, and budget rules explicit so future agents can reason about hits, misses, and evictions.

DONE WHEN:
Repeated content stops paying full derived-work cost on every refresh without risking stale or cross-version incorrectness.","acceptance_criteria":"ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Memoization keys combine stable packet hash with algorithm or version fingerprint so incompatible derivations cannot be silently reused.
- Cache behavior is operator-auditable through structured hit, miss, invalidation, eviction, quarantine, and budget logs, with explicit storage and retention rules.
- Unit, integration, and robot or E2E scenarios cover repeated-content refreshes, version bumps, cache invalidation, sampled recompute or compare-mode verification, suspected corruption or stale-entry quarantine, and bounded storage so memoization improves real workloads without silent stale-output risk.","notes":"LOCAL VALIDATION POLICY:
- This bead must land with bead-local proof, not only final-epic proof.
- Required by default: targeted unit tests for new invariants, integration tests for the affected runtime path, and at least one CLI or robot E2E scenario when the behavior is user-visible, multi-step, or crash-sensitive.
- Preserve rich structured timestamped logs, strategy or fallback reasons, and artifact snapshots relevant to the bead: manifests, benchmark tables, digests, checkpoints, crash-resume traces, controller decisions, or cache evidence.
- Prefer extending the shared harness from coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.15 and the crash or load harness from coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.17 instead of inventing ad hoc test entrypoints.
- Heavy cargo-driven builds, tests, benches, or profiling for this track must use rch.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:11.509404018Z","created_by":"RedCat","updated_at":"2026-04-23T15:43:09.195565970Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","caching","indexing","performance","semantic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:11.509404018Z","created_by":"RedCat","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.31","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:40:51.822523343Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.32","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:15.428873220Z","created_by":"RedCat","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":516,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34","author":"RedCat","text":"DESIGN / ACCEPTANCE / NOTES:
- Memoization is only worth doing for pure or version-fingerprinted derivations; do not cache hidden side effects.
- Budget and eviction policy must be explicit and inspectable, not mystery state.
- Prefer cache entries validated by packet hash and algorithm version rather than ad hoc heuristics.
- Acceptance requires observable cache hits, misses, and invalidation reasons plus proof that version changes cannot silently reuse incompatible outputs.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:11Z"},{"id":527,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34","author":"ubuntu","text":"POLISH ROUND 3:
- Added a direct dependency on coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.31 because stable packet hashes and packet-version semantics are not merely transitive implementation detail; they are the conceptual foundation of safe content-addressed memoization.
- The acceptance bar now explicitly includes cache auditability and repeated-content E2E scenarios so memoization serves users through faster refreshes without creating mystery state.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:40:55Z"},{"id":639,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34","author":"cc_2","text":"2026-04-22: Shipped content-addressed memoization vocabulary slice (commit 549e10f5).

What landed in src/indexer/memoization.rs (newly created):
- MemoContentHash — opaque byte-fingerprint carrier; producer-owned hasher choice.
- MemoKey { content_hash, algorithm, algorithm_version } — the core invariant: any version bump of any derivation invalidates its prior cache entries because the composite key differs. Silent stale cross-version reuse is impossible by construction.
- MemoLookup<V> — Hit / Miss / Quarantined variants, snake_case serialized for structured audit logs.
- MemoCacheEvent — Hit / Miss / Insert / Evict{CapacityLru|Invalidated} / Quarantine{reason} / Invalidate unified vocabulary for downstream logs.
- MemoCacheStats — hits, misses, inserts, evictions_capacity, invalidations, quarantined, live_entries counters; snake_case serializable.
- ContentAddressedMemoCache<V> — bounded LRU, quarantine-aware in-memory cache:
  - capacity_lru eviction when max_entries is reached (touched entries stay resident).
  - Quarantined entries remain resident (operator inspection) but never serve a hit; re-insertion over a quarantined key is a noop that returns Quarantine{reason}.
  - invalidate() returns true only when an entry was actually removed, so counter bumps are honest.

Nine unit tests, all passing:
- memo_key_distinguishes_by_content_algorithm_and_version
- memo_key_round_trips_through_json
- empty_cache_returns_miss_and_records_stat
- insert_then_get_returns_hit_and_bumps_counters
- version_bump_does_not_hit_prior_entry
- capacity_lru_evicts_oldest_and_reports_event
- invalidate_removes_entry_and_bumps_counter
- quarantined_entry_stays_resident_but_never_hits
- stats_serialize_as_snake_case_and_count_live_entries

Module is dead-code tolerated; nothing in the rebuild pipeline consumes it yet.

Bead stays in_progress. Remaining slices for closure:
- Wire ContentAddressedMemoCache into the lexical normalization, token extraction, and semantic-embedding paths once the ConversationPacket dataflow migration (ibuuh.32) lands and the hot derivations are factored through packets.
- Expose structured tracing::info!/warn! events for every MemoCacheEvent variant (hit/miss/insert/evict/quarantine/invalidate) at the wiring sites.
- Integration tests for the repeated-content refresh, version-bump invalidation, sampled recompute / compare-mode verification, and bounded-storage scenarios mandated by AC3.
- Robot / E2E scenario proving repeated refreshes on an unchanged corpus stop paying full derived-work cost.
","created_at":"2026-04-22T22:53:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34.1","title":"ibuuh.34.1: structured memoization audit records for refresh cache decisions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:44:11.469075580Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:56:54.346557130Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:56:54.346277315Z","close_reason":"Added structured memoization audit records and audited cache operation helpers with proof tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:44:11.469075580Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -1330,6 +1331,7 @@
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-x399","title":"Task 1: Design MessageGroup data structure for consolidated rendering","description":"# Objective
Create new data structures that represent GROUPED messages - a primary message plus all its associated tool calls and results.

## Current Problem
In lib.rs:10254-10302, each message becomes an individual Message struct. There's no concept of grouping.

## Design

### New Types (in renderer.rs)

```rust
/// Type of message group for rendering decisions
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum MessageGroupType {
    User,
    Assistant,
    System,
    ToolOnly,  // Orphan tool calls without parent message
}

/// A group of related messages for consolidated rendering.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct MessageGroup {
    /// Group type for rendering decisions
    pub group_type: MessageGroupType,
    /// The primary message (user or assistant text)
    pub primary: Message,
    /// Tool calls paired with their results
    pub tool_calls: Vec<ToolCallWithResult>,
    /// Timestamp range for the entire interaction
    pub start_timestamp: Option<String>,
    pub end_timestamp: Option<String>,
}

/// Tool call paired with its result for correlation
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct ToolCallWithResult {
    /// The original tool call
    pub call: ToolCall,
    /// The result (if received)
    pub result: Option<ToolResult>,
    /// Correlation ID (tool_use_id in Claude format)
    pub correlation_id: Option<String>,
}

/// Extended tool result with status and content
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct ToolResult {
    /// Tool name this responds to
    pub tool_name: String,
    /// Result content (may be truncated for display)
    pub content: String,
    /// Execution status
    pub status: ToolStatus,
    /// Correlation ID to match with call
    pub correlation_id: Option<String>,
}

impl MessageGroup {
    pub fn new(primary: Message, group_type: MessageGroupType) -> Self;
    pub fn add_tool_call(&mut self, call: ToolCall, correlation_id: Option<String>);
    pub fn add_tool_result(&mut self, result: ToolResult);
    pub fn tool_count(&self) -> usize;
    pub fn has_errors(&self) -> bool;
}
```

### Key Design Decisions
1. **Correlation by ID**: Claude uses tool_use_id to link calls/results
2. **Paired storage**: ToolCallWithResult keeps call+result together
3. **Timestamp range**: Group tracks start/end times
4. **Group type enum**: Different rendering for user/assistant/system

## Files to Modify
- src/html_export/renderer.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] MessageGroup struct with all fields
- [ ] MessageGroupType enum
- [ ] ToolCallWithResult for paired storage
- [ ] ToolResult struct

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-x4sj","title":"Implement remote index triggering","description":"# Implement remote index triggering

## What
After installing cass on a remote machine (or if it was already installed but 
never indexed), trigger the initial indexing process so that session data is 
ready to sync.

## Why
Syncing works by pulling from the remote's indexed data. If the remote has 
never run `cass index`, there's nothing meaningful to sync. The setup wizard 
should ensure remotes are indexed before attempting sync.

This also provides value to users who may not realize they need to index on 
each machine.

## Technical Design

### Index Status Detection
The probe phase already detects index status via `cass health --json`. This 
tells us:
- Whether index exists
- Session count
- Last index timestamp
- Index health

### Skip Logic
Skip indexing if:
- cass health reports healthy index with sessions
- Index timestamp is recent (< 24 hours)
- User explicitly passed --skip-index

### RemoteIndexer Implementation
```rust
pub struct RemoteIndexer {
    host: String,
    ssh_timeout: u64,
}

pub struct IndexProgress {
    pub stage: IndexStage,
    pub message: String,
    pub sessions_found: u64,
    pub sessions_indexed: u64,
}

pub enum IndexStage {
    Starting,
    Scanning { agent: String },
    Building,
    Complete,
    Failed { error: String },
}

pub struct IndexResult {
    pub success: bool,
    pub sessions_indexed: u64,
    pub duration: Duration,
    pub error: Option<String>,
}

impl RemoteIndexer {
    /// Check if indexing is needed based on probe result
    pub fn needs_indexing(probe: &HostProbeResult) -> bool {
        match &probe.cass_status {
            CassStatus::NotFound => true,  // Just installed, needs index
            CassStatus::InstalledNotIndexed { .. } => true,
            CassStatus::Indexed { session_count, .. } => *session_count == 0,
            CassStatus::Unknown => true,
        }
    }
    
    /// Run indexing on remote host
    pub async fn run_index(
        &self,
        on_progress: impl Fn(IndexProgress),
    ) -> Result<IndexResult, IndexError> {
        let start = Instant::now();
        
        on_progress(IndexProgress {
            stage: IndexStage::Starting,
            message: "Starting index...".into(),
            sessions_found: 0,
            sessions_indexed: 0,
        });
        
        // Run cass index with streaming output
        let result = self.run_ssh_command_streaming(
            "cass index --progress",
            |line| {
                if let Some(progress) = parse_index_progress(&line) {
                    on_progress(progress);
                }
            }
        ).await?;
        
        // Get final count
        let health = self.run_ssh_command("cass health --json").await?;
        let session_count = parse_session_count(&health);
        
        Ok(IndexResult {
            success: result.success,
            sessions_indexed: session_count,
            duration: start.elapsed(),
            error: result.error,
        })
    }
}
```

### Long-Running Index Handling
For hosts with many sessions (100k+), indexing can take 10+ minutes:

```rust
/// For long indexes, use background execution with polling
async fn run_long_index(&self, on_progress: impl Fn(IndexProgress)) -> Result<IndexResult> {
    // Start index in background
    self.run_ssh_command("nohup cass index > ~/.cass_index.log 2>&1 &").await?;
    
    // Poll progress
    loop {
        let log = self.run_ssh_command("tail -20 ~/.cass_index.log").await?;
        
        if let Some(progress) = parse_index_progress(&log) {
            on_progress(progress);
            
            if progress.stage == IndexStage::Complete {
                break;
            }
        }
        
        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(2)).await;
    }
    
    // Get final result
    self.get_index_result().await
}
```

### Progress Display
```
Indexing sessions on yto...
  Scanning ~/.claude/projects... found 234 sessions
  Scanning ~/.codex/sessions... found 12 sessions
  Scanning ~/.gemini/tmp... found 45 sessions
  
  Building search index...
  ████████████████████████████████████░░░░░░ 75% (219/291)
  
✓ Indexed 291 sessions on yto (45s)
```

### Error Handling
```rust
pub enum IndexError {
    SshFailed(String),
    CassNotFound,
    IndexFailed { 
        stdout: String, 
        stderr: String,
        exit_code: i32,
    },
    DiskFull,
    Timeout,
}

fn handle_index_error(error: &IndexError) -> String {
    match error {
        IndexError::DiskFull => {
            "Disk full on remote. Free space and retry."
        }
        IndexError::Timeout => {
            "Index timed out. Try running manually: ssh host 'cass index'"
        }
        IndexError::IndexFailed { stderr, .. } if stderr.contains("permission denied") => {
            "Permission error. Check file permissions in agent data directories."
        }
        _ => "Index failed. See error details above."
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detects whether indexing is needed from probe results
- [ ] Skips indexing if already indexed with sessions
- [ ] Triggers `cass index` on remote via SSH
- [ ] Streams indexing progress to terminal
- [ ] Reports session count after indexing
- [ ] Handles long-running indexes (10+ min) without timeout
- [ ] Handles failures gracefully with helpful messages
- [ ] Works with freshly-installed cass

## Dependencies
- Requires: Remote installation (coding_agent_session_search-o6ax) - if cass wasn't installed
- Requires: SSH probing (coding_agent_session_search-vxe2) - to know if indexing needed

## Edge Cases
- Very large session history (100k+ sessions) → use background + polling
- Disk fills up during index → detect and report
- Index partially completes → should be resumable via `cass index`
- User cancels during index → remote index continues, can check later","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-05T13:08:15.203886Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-05T16:19:50.653795Z","closed_at":"2026-01-05T16:19:50.653795Z","close_reason":"Implemented RemoteIndexer in src/sources/index.rs with needs_indexing(), run_index(), progress streaming, and nohup+polling for long-running indexes (commit f083d68)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["indexing","sources","ssh"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-x4sj","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-o6ax","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-x4sj","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-vxe2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-x4xb","title":"P5.3: Safety Confirmations","description":"# P5.3: Safety Confirmations

## Goal
Implement a multi-step confirmation flow that ensures users explicitly acknowledge the implications of publishing encrypted content to a public GitHub Pages site, preventing accidental or uninformed publishing.

## Background & Rationale

### Why Confirmation Gates Matter
Publishing to GitHub Pages is:
1. **Public**: Anyone with the URL can access the encrypted archive
2. **Persistent**: GitHub retains history; deletion is not immediate
3. **Indexable**: Search engines may discover and cache the URL
4. **Irreversible**: Once published, copies may exist elsewhere

### Behavioral Safeguards
Users should not be able to "click through" without reading. The confirmation flow should:
1. Require deliberate action (not just pressing Enter)
2. Display specific warnings tailored to the users configuration
3. Provide clear abort options at every stage
4. Confirm understanding, not just acceptance

## Technical Implementation

### Confirmation Steps

```rust
pub enum ConfirmationStep {
    SecretScanAcknowledgment,
    ContentReview,
    PublicPublishingWarning,
    PasswordStrengthConfirmation,
    RecoveryKeyBackup,
    FinalConfirmation,
}

pub struct ConfirmationFlow {
    current_step: ConfirmationStep,
    completed_steps: HashSet<ConfirmationStep>,
    export_config: ExportConfig,
    summary: PrePublishSummary,
}

impl ConfirmationFlow {
    pub fn next_step(&mut self) -> Option<ConfirmationStep> {
        match self.current_step {
            ConfirmationStep::SecretScanAcknowledgment => {
                if self.summary.secret_scan.has_findings() {
                    Some(ConfirmationStep::SecretScanAcknowledgment)
                } else {
                    // Skip if no secrets found
                    self.advance_to(ConfirmationStep::ContentReview)
                }
            }
            // ... handle other transitions
        }
    }
    
    pub fn can_proceed(&self) -> bool {
        self.completed_steps.contains(&ConfirmationStep::FinalConfirmation)
    }
}
```

### Step 1: Secret Scan Acknowledgment

Only shown if secrets were detected:

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ⚠️  SECRETS DETECTED                                         │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                              │

│                                                              │

│ • 8 MEDIUM findings (potential passwords)                    │

│                                                              │

│                                                              │

│                                                              │
│ [V] View findings  [E] Exclude content  [A] Abort           │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

The user must type the exact phrase to proceed.

User must type the last word to prove they read it.

Require two distinct keypresses to prevent accidental triggering.

### Implementation

```rust
pub struct ConfirmationUI {
    flow: ConfirmationFlow,
    input_buffer: String,
    error_message: Option<String>,
}

impl ConfirmationUI {
    pub fn handle_input(&mut self, key: KeyEvent) -> ConfirmationResult {
        match key.code {
            KeyCode::Char(c) => {
                self.input_buffer.push(c);
                ConfirmationResult::Continue
            }
            KeyCode::Enter => {
                if self.validate_current_step() {
                    self.flow.complete_current_step();
                    if self.flow.can_proceed() {
                        ConfirmationResult::Confirmed
                    } else {
                        self.flow.advance();
                        ConfirmationResult::Continue
                    }
                } else {
                    self.error_message = Some(self.get_validation_error());
                    ConfirmationResult::Continue
                }
            }
            KeyCode::Char('a') if key.modifiers.is_empty() => {
                ConfirmationResult::Aborted
            }
            _ => ConfirmationResult::Continue
        }
    }
    
    fn validate_current_step(&self) -> bool {
        match self.flow.current_step {
            ConfirmationStep::SecretScanAcknowledgment => {
                self.input_buffer.to_lowercase() == "i understand the risks"
            }
            ConfirmationStep::PublicPublishingWarning => {
                let expected = format!("publish to {}", self.flow.export_config.target_domain);
                self.input_buffer.to_lowercase() == expected.to_lowercase()
            }
            ConfirmationStep::RecoveryKeyBackup => {
                let last_word = self.flow.recovery_key.split('-').last().unwrap_or("");
                self.input_buffer.to_lowercase() == last_word.to_lowercase()
            }
            // ... other validations
        }
    }
}
```

### Abort at Any Stage

Every screen must have a clear abort option that:
1. Confirms the user wants to abort
2. Explains what happens (nothing published, local files cleaned up)
3. Returns to main menu

```rust
fn handle_abort(&mut self) -> ConfirmationResult {
    // Show confirmation
    let confirmed = self.show_abort_confirmation();
    if confirmed {
        // Clean up any temporary files
        self.cleanup_temp_files();
        ConfirmationResult::Aborted
    } else {
        ConfirmationResult::Continue
    }
}
```

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-x8sl","title":"Performance and Load Testing Suite","description":"# Performance and Load Testing Suite

## What
Create a performance testing suite that measures and tracks:
- Search query latency (P50, P95, P99)
- Indexing throughput (sessions/second)
- Memory usage under load
- Concurrent search behavior
- Large dataset handling

## Why
cass needs to perform well with:
- Large session histories (10,000+ sessions)
- Concurrent searches
- Large individual sessions (1MB+ of content)
- Fast startup time

Without benchmarks, we cannot detect performance regressions.

## Technical Design

### Using Criterion for Benchmarks
```toml
# Cargo.toml
[dev-dependencies]
criterion = { version = "*", features = ["html_reports"] }

[[bench]]
name = "search_benchmarks"
harness = false

[[bench]]
name = "indexer_benchmarks"
harness = false
```

### Search Benchmarks
```rust
// benches/search_benchmarks.rs
use criterion::{black_box, criterion_group, criterion_main, BenchmarkId, Criterion};
use coding_agent_search::search::SearchEngine;
use std::time::Duration;

fn bench_search_simple_query(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let engine = setup_test_engine(1000); // 1000 sessions
    
    c.bench_function("search_simple", |b| {
        b.iter(|| {
            engine.search(black_box("authentication error"), 10)
        })
    });
}

fn bench_search_complex_query(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let engine = setup_test_engine(1000);
    
    c.bench_function("search_complex", |b| {
        b.iter(|| {
            engine.search(black_box("(auth OR login) AND error"), 10)
        })
    });
}

fn bench_search_scaling(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let mut group = c.benchmark_group("search_scaling");
    
    for size in [100, 1000, 10000].iter() {
        let engine = setup_test_engine(*size);
        
        group.bench_with_input(
            BenchmarkId::new("sessions", size),
            size,
            |b, _| {
                b.iter(|| engine.search(black_box("test query"), 10))
            }
        );
    }
    
    group.finish();
}

fn bench_concurrent_search(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let engine = Arc::new(setup_test_engine(1000));
    
    c.bench_function("search_concurrent_4", |b| {
        b.iter(|| {
            let handles: Vec<_> = (0..4)
                .map(|i| {
                    let e = engine.clone();
                    std::thread::spawn(move || {
                        e.search(&format!("query {}", i), 10)
                    })
                })
                .collect();
            
            for h in handles {
                h.join().unwrap();
            }
        })
    });
}

criterion_group!(
    benches,
    bench_search_simple_query,
    bench_search_complex_query,
    bench_search_scaling,
    bench_concurrent_search,
);
criterion_main!(benches);
```

### Indexer Benchmarks
```rust
// benches/indexer_benchmarks.rs
use criterion::{black_box, criterion_group, criterion_main, Criterion};
use coding_agent_search::indexer::Indexer;
use tempfile::TempDir;

fn bench_index_sessions(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let sessions = generate_test_sessions(100);
    
    c.bench_function("index_100_sessions", |b| {
        b.iter_with_setup(
            || {
                let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
                let indexer = Indexer::new(tmp.path()).unwrap();
                (tmp, indexer, sessions.clone())
            },
            |(tmp, indexer, sessions)| {
                for session in sessions {
                    indexer.index_session(black_box(&session)).unwrap();
                }
            }
        )
    });
}

fn bench_reindex_full(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let mut group = c.benchmark_group("reindex");
    group.sample_size(10); // Fewer samples for slow operation
    group.measurement_time(Duration::from_secs(60));
    
    group.bench_function("full_reindex_1000", |b| {
        b.iter_with_setup(
            || setup_populated_index(1000),
            |indexer| indexer.reindex_full()
        )
    });
    
    group.finish();
}

criterion_group!(
    benches,
    bench_index_sessions,
    bench_reindex_full,
);
criterion_main!(benches);
```

### Memory Tests
```rust
// tests/memory_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_memory_usage_search() {
    let engine = setup_test_engine(10000);
    
    // Get baseline memory
    let baseline = get_process_memory();
    
    // Run many searches
    for i in 0..1000 {
        engine.search(&format!("query {}", i), 10).unwrap();
    }
    
    let after = get_process_memory();
    let growth = after - baseline;
    
    // Should not grow significantly (no memory leak)
    assert!(
        growth < 10_000_000, // 10MB
        "Memory grew by {} bytes during search loop",
        growth
    );
}

fn get_process_memory() -> usize {
    // Read /proc/self/statm on Linux
    #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
    {
        let statm = std::fs::read_to_string("/proc/self/statm").unwrap();
        let pages: usize = statm.split_whitespace().next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        pages * 4096
    }
    #[cfg(not(target_os = "linux"))]
    { 0 }
}
```

### Performance CI Check
```yaml
# .github/workflows/bench.yml
name: Benchmarks

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:

jobs:
  benchmark:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-action@stable
        
      - name: Run benchmarks
        run: cargo bench --bench search_benchmarks -- --save-baseline main
        
      - name: Upload benchmark results
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: criterion-report
          path: target/criterion
```

### Benchmark Report Script
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/bench-report.sh

echo "Running performance benchmarks..."
cargo bench --bench search_benchmarks --bench indexer_benchmarks 2>&1 | tee bench-output.txt

echo ""
echo "Results saved to target/criterion/"
echo "Open target/criterion/report/index.html for detailed reports"

# Extract key metrics
echo ""
echo "Key Metrics:"
grep -E "time:.*\[" bench-output.txt | head -10
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Criterion benchmarks for search operations
- [ ] Criterion benchmarks for indexing operations
- [ ] Memory profiling tests (no leaks)
- [ ] Concurrent search stress test
- [ ] HTML benchmark reports generated
- [ ] CI tracks benchmark results
- [ ] scripts/bench-report.sh works locally
- [ ] Benchmarks complete in < 5 minutes

## Dependencies
- criterion crate
- heaptrack (optional, for memory profiling)

## Considerations
- Run benchmarks on consistent hardware in CI
- Use --save-baseline for comparison
- Sample size affects accuracy vs time
- Separate bench from test for faster feedback

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-x9fd","title":"P2.2: AES-256-GCM Streaming Encryption","description":"# P2.2: AES-256-GCM Streaming Encryption

## Goal
Implement streaming envelope encryption: compress -> chunk -> encrypt -> write, with O(1) memory usage. Output is always chunked AEAD ciphertext under payload/ plus config.json.

## Hard Requirements
- Default chunk size: 8 MiB (configurable).
- Hard cap: 32 MiB (avoid GH Pages warnings and large-file limits).
- No single encrypted.bin or archive.enc output.
- Compression BEFORE encryption (deflate default; optional zstd; none for debug).
- All outputs must be streamable (no full-file buffering).

## Output Artifacts

```
site/
  config.json
  payload/
    chunk-00000.bin
    chunk-00001.bin
    ...
```

config.json includes: version, export_id, base_nonce, compression, kdf defaults, payload.chunk_size, payload.chunk_count, payload.files[], key_slots[] (slot_type, kdf, salt, nonce, wrapped_dek), exported_at, cass_version.

## Crypto Design
- AES-256-GCM for payload chunks and key slot wrapping.
- export_id (16 bytes) and base_nonce (12 bytes) generated per export.
- Per-chunk nonce derived from base_nonce + counter.
- AAD binds export_id + chunk_index (and optionally chunk_len) to prevent swapping.
- Key slot wrapping uses per-slot nonce; AAD binds export_id + slot_id.

## Compression Options
- deflate (default, fflate in browser)
- zstd (optional)
- none (debug / reproducible tests)

## Test Requirements

### Unit Tests
- chunk_size enforcement (reject > 32 MiB)
- compression round-trip for deflate/zstd/none
- AAD tampering causes decrypt failure
- key slot unwrap failure on wrong password

### Integration Tests
- encrypt -> decrypt -> byte-for-byte match
- large payload streaming (no O(n) memory growth)
- payload.files list matches emitted chunks

### E2E Script
- Build sample export, encrypt, then verify via cass pages --verify
- Log per-phase timing: compress, encrypt, write

## Files to Create/Modify
- src/pages/encrypt.rs
- src/pages/config.rs
- tests/pages_encrypt.rs
- tests/fixtures/pages_encrypt/

## Exit Criteria
1. Streaming encryption works for large exports
2. Chunk size defaults and limits enforced
3. config.json matches payload output
4.
